issues.comment: The `getCredentials()` method creates a `RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialModel` via `createFromValues()` but never sets the ID on the returned model. The stored credential has an ID (generated in `updateCredential()` at line 196), but it is not propagated to the model returned by `getCredentials()`. This results in `getId()` returning null. Downstream, when `RecoveryAuthnCodesFormAuthenticator` calls `removeStoredCredentialById(recoveryCodeCredentialModel.getId())` after all codes are used, it passes null, causing either silent failure or a NullPointerException. Fix: add `model.setId(myUser.recoveryCodes.getId());` after the `createFromValues` call.
